title: rorippa portoricensis subsp. pumila
intro: Rorippa portoricensis subsp. pumila is a subspecies of the cruciferous plant Rorippa, also known as the Puerto Rican watercress or dwarf watercress. It is a perennial herb native to Puerto Rico and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various conditions. Rorippa portoricensis subsp. pumila can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often consumed raw in salads or as a supplement.
